page: Alibaba Cloud ecs.r5.2xlarge is a memory-optimized virtual machine instance powered by the KVM hypervisor. It features 8 dedicated vCPUs mapped to 8 physical cores using an Intel Xeon 8163 processor running at 2.5 GHz. The x86_64 architecture includes a 35 MB L3 cache, 1.0 MB L2 cache, and 32 KB L1d cache. It is equipped with 64.0 GB of system memory, yielding a high memory-to-core ratio of 8.0 GB per core. The instance does not include local storage or dedicated GPU hardware, and it provides a baseline network bandwidth of 2 Gbps.

Performance benchmarks indicate a moderate to weak processing profile. Single-core and multi-core CPU performance tests, such as stress-ng and Geekbench, place the instance in the bottom 25% tier. However, memory bandwidth for large uncached blocks performs in the average range, reaching 41.01 GB/sec for read operations and 35.87 GB/sec for write operations. Cryptographic and extended instruction sets perform at an average level, supported by hardware flags like AVX-512 and AES. Conversely, single-threaded compression and decompression tasks show poor performance, falling into the bottom 10% tier.

From a cost-efficiency perspective, the ecs.r5.2xlarge offers a high-density memory allocation relative to its compute resources, making it a viable option for memory-bound workloads that do not require intensive CPU performance. Given the 8:1 RAM-to-vCPU ratio and average large-block memory bandwidth, this instance is suited for caching layers, in-memory databases with moderate transaction volumes, and lightweight enterprise applications. It is less suitable for compute-heavy tasks, high-throughput web serving, or large-scale database operations due to its weaker CPU and database benchmark results.
description: Alibaba Cloud ecs.r5.2xlarge is a memory-optimized instance featuring 8 dedicated vCPUs and 64.0 GB of RAM, running on an Intel Xeon 8163 processor at 2.5 GHz. Utilizing the KVM hypervisor and x86_64 architecture, it delivers a high memory-to-core ratio of 8.0 GB per core. The instance lacks local storage and GPUs, relying on a baseline network bandwidth of 2 Gbps. Benchmark data shows weak single-core and multi-core CPU performance, alongside poor single-threaded compression speeds. However, it maintains average performance in large-block uncached memory bandwidth (41.01 GB/sec read) and LLM inference for small models. Qualitatively, this instance provides cost-effective memory density for workloads that prioritize RAM capacity over raw processing power. It is best suited for memory-intensive applications such as caching layers, moderate in-memory databases, and general-purpose enterprise workloads with low CPU demands.
og_description: Alibaba Cloud ecs.r5.2xlarge is a memory-optimized instance with 8 Intel Xeon vCPUs, 64 GB RAM, and a 2 Gbps network baseline, designed for memory-intensive workloads requiring moderate compute.
meta_description: Alibaba Cloud ecs.r5.2xlarge features 8 Intel Xeon vCPUs and 64 GB RAM, offering a memory-optimized profile for caching and database workloads.
tagline: A memory-optimized virtual server delivering high RAM density and dedicated Intel Xeon cores for memory-bound enterprise workloads.
bullet_points: 8 dedicated Intel Xeon 8163 vCPUs running at 2.5 GHz
64.0 GB of RAM providing an 8:1 memory-to-vCPU ratio
Average large-block uncached memory read bandwidth of 41.01 GB/sec
No local storage or GPU accelerators included
Best suited for caching layers and moderate in-memory databases
